title: discaria crenata var. integrifolia
intro: Discaria crenata var. integrifolia, also known as the needle bush, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rhamnaceae. It is native to Australia and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate pain, reduce fever, and combat infections. In traditional medicine, the leaves and stems of the needle bush are often prepared as a tea or infused in oil for use in topical preparations, such as creams and ointments.
